vocab.example: I decided to follow in my father's footsteps and study Medicine, as I've been inspired by his dedication to healing people.

vocab.meaning: thế giới mở rộng trước mắt/có mọi cơ hội
vocab.example: I chose Business Administration because I felt that with such a versatile degree, the world would be my oyster in terms of career paths.

vocab.meaning: tiếng gọi từ bên trong/đam mê mãnh liệt như định mệnh
vocab.example: For me, Social Work wasn't just a choice; it felt like a calling because I've always had a profound desire to advocate for the marginalized.
I opted for Philosophy because I wanted a major that would truly broaden my mind and challenge my preconceived notions about reality.

vocab.word: get your foot in the door
vocab.meaning: bước chân vào một lĩnh vực/tổ chức (tạo tiền đề)
vocab.example: I picked Information Technology primarily to get my foot in the door of the booming tech industry.

vocab.meaning: ở vị trí tiên phong/dẫn đầu
vocab.example: Biotechnology appealed to me because I wanted to be at the forefront of medical innovations and genetic research.

vocab.meaning: tạo cho ai đó một lợi thế cạnh tranh
vocab.example: I believe that mastering Data Analytics will give me a significant edge in the competitive job market after graduation.

vocab.meaning: giữ cho các lựa chọn luôn rộng mở (không gò bó)
vocab.example: I chose Economics because it’s a broad field that allows me to keep my options open for both public and private sector roles.
A guest lecture on sustainability gave me a lot of food for thought, which eventually led me to change my major to Environmental Science.

vocab.meaning: chi tiết từ trong ra ngoài/mọi khía cạnh
vocab.example: I've always wanted to learn the ins and outs of how global markets function, so Finance was the natural choice for me.
